compositional_truth_check.p1_yellow_pot: Present, soil surface visible, no visible foliage.
compositional_truth_check.p2_p4_black_pot_shared: Present, contains single stem/sprout and eggshell debris.
compositional_truth_check.p3_black_pot_rabbit: Present, contains single leaf specimen and white rabbit anchor.
systemic_losses: P1 (String of Nickels) appears to be a systemic loss; no foliage detected in the yellow pot.
P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) shows severe decline; only a single, thin, etiolated stem remains.
new_introductions: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing in the black pots.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is in a state of advanced senescence or failure. Only P3 retains a single viable-looking leaf.

plant_audit.p2_p4: Single, thin, pale green stem. High probability of etiolation.
plant_audit.p3: Single, broad, dark green leaf. Appears stable but isolated.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, appears consistently moist/damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in black pots. White rabbit anchor in P3 is stable.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: Low-light stress is evident; lack of structural growth suggests insufficient photosynthesis.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Minimal change; P3 leaf remains, P2/P4 stem is present.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-1: Gradual loss of turgor in the P2/P4 stem; the stem appears to be leaning or collapsing further.
temporal_deltas.current_state: The P2/P4 stem has lost significant verticality compared to the earliest image.

anomalies: Presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at calcium supplementation or pH adjustment.
Extreme etiolation of the P2/P4 specimen indicates the current light source is inadequate for the species' metabolic needs.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is suffering from severe light deprivation and potential root-zone issues. P1 is effectively a bare pot. P2/P4 is struggling to maintain structural integrity, with the stem showing signs of collapse. P3 is the only specimen maintaining a healthy leaf, though it is not showing signs of active growth. The environment is static and failing to support the expected biome.
